---
title: "Pygo_gene_enrichment"
author: "Galen O'Shea-Stone"
date: "2025-06-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
##############################################################################
## Transcriptomics → GO / KEGG → Reactome enrichment (Gallus gallus)        ##
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ##
##  • Input : CSV with columns  gene_id , log2FC , padj                    ##
##  • Output: Excel with ORA & GSEA tables, PDF dot-plots, optional KEGG   ##
##            pathway diagrams coloured by log2FC                          ##
##############################################################################

## 0.  CONFIGURATION  =========================================================
# Change `input_file` to the path of your CSV file
input_file   <- "pygo_MAIN.csv"      # ← change to your file name
# (Ensure the CSV columns are in order: gene symbol, log2 fold change, adjusted p-value)
species_name <- "chicken"            # ReactomePA expects "chicken" for Gallus gallus
kegg_code    <- "gga"                # 3-letter KEGG code for Gallus gallus
padj_cutoff  <- 0.05                 # threshold for defining significant genes

## 1.  SETUP  -----------------------------------------------------------------
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager")
}

## ── CRAN extras required by clusterProfiler (network plots, tree plots, pie charts)
cran_extras <- c(
  "ggraph",      # for network/emap plotting
  "igraph",      # underlying graph engine
  "graphlayouts",# layout algorithms for ggraph
  "ggforce",     # specialized geoms used by clusterProfiler
  "ape",         # phylogenetic tree utilities used by clusterProfiler
  "scatterpie"   # pie charts on enrichment trees
)
need_cran <- cran_extras[!sapply(cran_extras, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(need_cran)) {
  install.packages(need_cran, dependencies = TRUE)
}

## ── Bioconductor core packages  --------------------------------------------
bio_pkgs <- c(
  "clusterProfiler",
  "ReactomePA",
  "pathview",
  "biomaRt",
  "AnnotationDbi",
  "dplyr",
  "ggplot2",
  "openxlsx"
)
need_bio <- bio_pkgs[!sapply(bio_pkgs, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(need_bio)) {
  BiocManager::install(need_bio, update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)
}

## ── OrgDb for Gallus gallus  ------------------------------------------------
# This installs the OrgDb package if it’s not already present
if (!requireNamespace("org.Gg.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {
  BiocManager::install("org.Gg.eg.db", update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)
}

## ── Load all libraries  ----------------------------------------------------
library(clusterProfiler)
library(ReactomePA)
library(pathview)
library(biomaRt)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(openxlsx)
library(org.Gg.eg.db)

# Assign the OrgDb object for later calls
orgDb <- org.Gg.eg.db

# Create output directory if it doesn’t already exist
dir.create("output", showWarnings = FALSE)

## 2.  READ DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION TABLE  ------------------------------------
tbl <- read.csv(input_file, check.names = FALSE) %>%
       dplyr::rename(gene = 1, logFC = 2, padj = 3)

## 3.  ID-MAPPING  (Gene Symbol → Entrez)  ------------------------------------
# Map from SYMBOL to ENTREZID using the OrgDb
gene2entrez <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  orgDb,
  keys    = tbl$gene,
  columns = "ENTREZID",
  keytype = "SYMBOL"
)

# Join back to the main table and drop any rows without an Entrez ID
tbl <- dplyr::left_join(tbl, gene2entrez, by = c("gene" = "SYMBOL")) %>%
       dplyr::filter(!is.na(ENTREZID))

# Define the universe and significant gene vectors (as character)
gene_universe <- tbl$ENTREZID %>% as.character()
sig_genes     <- tbl %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(padj < padj_cutoff) %>% 
                 dplyr::pull(ENTREZID) %>% 
                 as.character()

## 4.  OVER-REPRESENTATION ANALYSIS (ORA)  -------------------------------------
## 4a.  GO Biological Process (BP)
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe,
  OrgDb         = orgDb,
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable      = TRUE
)

## 4b.  KEGG ORA (Gallus gallus = "gga")
ekegg <- enrichKEGG(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe,
  organism      = kegg_code,
  keyType       = "ncbi-geneid",   # default, but explicit here
  pAdjustMethod = "BH"
)
# Check if KEGG returned any results
if (is.null(ekegg) || nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) == 0) {
  warning("enrichKEGG returned no pathways. 
          Verify that your Entrez IDs exist in KEGG for '", kegg_code, "'.")
} else {
  message("✅ enrichKEGG succeeded with ", nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)), " pathways.")
}

## 4c.  Reactome ORA (Gallus gallus → "chicken")
ereact <- enrichPathway(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe,
  organism      = species_name,      # must be "chicken" for Gallus gallus
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable      = TRUE
)
# Check if ReactomePA returned any results
if (is.null(ereact) || nrow(as.data.frame(ereact)) == 0) {
  warning("enrichPathway returned no pathways. 
          Ensure that '", species_name, "' is a supported organism in ReactomePA.")
} else {
  message("✅ enrichPathway succeeded with ", nrow(as.data.frame(ereact)), " pathways.")
}

## 5.  GSEA (preranked on log2FC)  --------------------------------------------
ranks <- tbl %>%
         dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(logFC)) %>%
         dplyr::mutate(rnk = logFC) %>%
         dplyr::select(ENTREZID, rnk) %>%
         tibble::deframe()

gsea_go <- gseGO(
  geneList      = ranks,
  OrgDb         = orgDb,
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH"
)
# Check if GSEA returned any results
if (is.null(gsea_go) || nrow(as.data.frame(gsea_go)) == 0) {
  warning("gseGO returned no significant pathways. 
          Try relaxing thresholds or verify your ranked gene list.")
} else {
  message("✅ gseGO succeeded with ", nrow(as.data.frame(gsea_go)), " pathways.")
}

## 6.  SAVE TABLES TO EXCEL  --------------------------------------------------
openxlsx::write.xlsx(
  list(
    GO_ORA   = if (!is.null(ego)     && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0)   as.data.frame(ego)   else data.frame(),
    KEGG_ORA = if (!is.null(ekegg)   && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) as.data.frame(ekegg) else data.frame(),
    Reactome = if (!is.null(ereact)  && nrow(as.data.frame(ereact)) > 0)as.data.frame(ereact)else data.frame(),
    GO_GSEA  = if (!is.null(gsea_go) && nrow(as.data.frame(gsea_go)) > 0)as.data.frame(gsea_go)else data.frame()
  ),
  file      = "output/enrichment_results.xlsx",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

## 7.  QUICK DOT-PLOTS  -------------------------------------------------------
pdf("output/dotplots.pdf", width = 9, height = 6)

if (!is.null(ego) && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0) {
  dotplot(ego, showCategory = 15, title = "GO BP ORA")
} else {
  message("⚠️ Skipping GO dotplot: no enriched terms.")
}

if (!is.null(ekegg) && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) {
  dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 15, title = "KEGG ORA")
} else {
  message("⚠️ Skipping KEGG dotplot: no enriched pathways.")
}

if (!is.null(ereact) && nrow(as.data.frame(ereact)) > 0) {
  dotplot(ereact, showCategory = 15, title = "Reactome ORA")
} else {
  message("⚠️ Skipping Reactome dotplot: no enriched pathways.")
}

if (!is.null(gsea_go) && nrow(as.data.frame(gsea_go)) > 0) {
  dotplot(gsea_go, showCategory = 15, title = "GO GSEA (preranked)")
} else {
  message("⚠️ Skipping GSEA dotplot: no significant pathways.")
}

dev.off()

## 8.  OPTIONAL: COLOUR THE TOP KEGG PATHWAY  ---------------------------------
if (!is.null(ekegg) && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) {
  top_pathway <- ekegg$ID[1]
  pathview(
    gene.data   = tbl$logFC %>% setNames(tbl$ENTREZID),
    pathway.id  = top_pathway,
    species     = kegg_code,
    out.suffix  = "logFC",
    kegg.native = TRUE
  )
  message("✅ KEGG diagram for pathway ", top_pathway, " saved.")
}

cat("\n✓ All done!  Check the 'output/' folder for Excel & PDF results.\n")

```
